The members we read

The cities we read.

39 Metropolis members · 40 strategic plans, across six world regions — read in their own words.

02 · 08
Act I · The common language

Worlds apart, cities reach for the same things.

38 of 40 cities converge on the same core priorities — from Barcelona to São Paulo, the strategic language is remarkably shared.

Climate resiliencemitigation · adaptation · nature
95%
Housing accesssupply · tenure · affordability
90%
Digital transformationservices · data · innovation
88%
03 · 08
Act I · The aspirational trap

We each measure our own targets.
We can't yet read them together.

The ambition is shared. The measure is not.

Only 8 of 40 attach a comparable figure, and in units that can't even be added up.

04 · 08
Act II · From ambition to action

We didn't sample opinions.
We read what your cities committed.

From the plan
"...expand access to decent and affordable housing for vulnerable families and regularise informal settlements..."
City strategic plan · read in its original language
→
Objective
Housing access
Action type
Settlement regularisation
Comparable indicator
Households with secure tenure
Climate
"…create 160 hectares of new urban green space, prioritising areas with the greatest deficit." — a city Climate Plan
→
Hectares of new green space

Read by meaning across languages and formats, each plan maps to a structure the whole network shares.

05 · 08
Act II · From ambition to action

Where the network is moving, and where it isn't yet.

Some answers are already inside the network. Jakarta runs flood early-warning today. Taipei extends the same approach to heat, protecting seniors and outdoor workers.

06 · 08
Act III · Collective impact

If all 40 reported alike.

Even from the few cities that already quantify their commitments, the totals add up — and most of the network isn't counted yet.

Housing8 of 40 cities attach a comparable figure
≈113,000homes committed · +≈260,000 households via tenure
Climate3 of 40 cities attach a comparable figure
≈10 km²of new urban green space — Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Amman
A floor, not the ceiling  a shared measure would let the whole network count.
07 · 08
Act III · The observatory

Imagine it on MetroFutures.

Every plan, theme and commitment we read — climate, housing and digital — in one shared, living view of the whole network.

Explore the live platform →

From tracking our own targets
to reading our impact together.

We already share the common language.
Let's build the common measure together.
